item.title: Version 0.1.12
item.description:
What's new?
Create Notes: You can now create local Markdown files (aka notes) from ArcMark and edit them directly in the browser. Everything is local-first; newly added files can be organised exactly the same way as you organise your links.
You can even create a new note by copying some text into the clipboard and clicking "Add links from clipboard". ArcMark will detect that you only have plain text and, instead of saving a link, it will create a new note.
Agent Skills: Install the ArcMark skill and manage every workspace and link directly from your AI agent of choice. The skill is bundled with a minimal script that talks to ArcMark and lets your agent use the app just as you would.
Reminders: Resurface links in the future by setting a reminder date. Reminders are always shown at the top of the selected workspace.
Other Fixes - Add overscroll space below the list of links and folders so it doesn't feel too crammed.

item.title: Version 0.1.10
item.description: Fixes: - When the Arcmark window is in attachment mode and is also hidden, it no longer captures gesture events. - Fixed a bug where the Arcmark window would break the focus for other windows when switching between apps using Command+Tab.

item.title: Version 0.1.9
item.description:
New: - Added horizontal swipe gesture to switch workspaces. It's off by default and can be enabled from Settings. - Added the ability to set a custom favicon for saved links. Select emoji, generic icons, or set the favicon from previously saved websites.
Fixes: - Hide tooltip during drag-and-drop reordering. - Group selected links into a folder at their common parent level. - Improve label truncation width in links on hover state.

item.title: Version 0.1.8
item.description: - Added support for the experimental browser profile in Helium browser. You can now map the workspace in Arcmar to the Helium profile, and all links from this workspace will open in that profile. - Added keyboard shortcuts to navigate between workspace in ArcMark (⌥⌘← / ⌥⌘→) - Added an automatic fallback for the sidebar position. Now, if there isn't enough space for the attached sidebar on the selected side, it will temporarily move to the opposite side to remain accessible. - Added a custom URL tooltip on link hover. It can be disabled from the settings. - Fixed URL text field wrapping in edit alert dialog

item.title: Version 0.1.7
item.description:
- Added **experimental support for separate profiles in Chrome and Firefox**. You can now set a custom profile from the Settings page when hovering over a workspace name. Once you do, all links in that workspace will be opened in the specific profile. - Added 'Open All Links' context menu action for folders to open multiple links at once. It also works when you select multiple links while holding the CMD key.
- Restore window size and position on relaunch. - Fix support for multi-window attachment for the same browsers. Now the app should correctly transition between all active windows of a selected browser.
